task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace GUI is visible on the screen with a new or active project.

task_completion.description: A closed shape with 12 segments is drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch of a rectangle with square notches removed from all four corners (forming exactly 12 horizontal and vertical line segments) is visible in the workspace.

task_completion.description: The profile is centered at the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drawn shape is symmetrically placed around the origin (the intersection of the thick reference axes) in the SolveSpace workspace.

task_completion.description: Overall bounding dimensions are constrained to 80x50mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Distance constraints of '80' (for the overall width) and '50' (for the overall height) are visible on the sketch.
Distance constraints of '5' are visible on the notch segments, ensuring the removed corners are 5mm x 5mm.

task_completion.description: The file is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The window title bar shows 'notched_panel.slvs' or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/notched_panel.slvs'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the SolveSpace GUI to create the sketch.

integrity.description: Agent did not write the .slvs file manually in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are open with the .slvs file contents.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to generate or download the .slvs file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show commands (like curl, wget, python, or echo) directly creating or downloading the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a SolveSpace file saved at `/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/notched_panel.slvs`. The sketch must be a closed 2D profile of exactly 12 line segments. The bounding box dimensions must be 80.0mm wide by 50.0mm tall. There must be 5.0mm x 5.0mm square notches removed from all four corners. The profile must be centered at the origin (0,0).
